metadata.title: The Solve-Verify Gap: Speed Widens the Asymmetry

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The domain ratios stuck with me — legal at seventy-two thousand x, finance at ten thousand eight hundred, and the code-with-tests exception where verify time drops at or below produce time. I want to turn that exception into a design principle. Help me: (1) take one of my own AI-assisted tasks and estimate its solve and verify times honestly, (2) design an automated verification layer that runs in seconds the way a test suite does, and (3) have Claude tell me whether my task even ADMITS such a layer or is structurally verification-bound.

props.command: The domain ratios stuck with me — legal at seventy-two thousand x, finance at ten thousand eight hundred, and the code-with-tests exception where verify time drops at or below produce time. I want to turn that exception into a design principle. Help me: (1) take one of my own AI-assisted tasks and estimate its solve and verify times honestly, (2) design an automated verification layer that runs in seconds the way a test suite does, and (3) have Claude tell me whether my task even ADMITS such a layer or is structurally verification-bound.
props.segment: The Solve-Verify Gap: Speed Widens the

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.
AI solves in seconds. Verifying one output can take hours of expert labor. The asymmetry widens as models get faster. The gate is human-sized.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'Solve: seconds. Verify: hours. The gate is human-sized.'

beats.narration_text: Can Claude quantify the solve-verify ratio across legal, medical, and finance domains?

props.command: claude "For legal, medical, and finance AI outputs: estimate typical AI produce time, expert verify time, ratio, and failure rate if verification is skipped. Cite sources."

beats.narration_text: Legal: 0.1 seconds to produce, 2 hours to verify — a ratio of 72000x. Medical: 2 seconds to produce, 4 hours to verify — 7200x. Finance: 1 second, 3 hours — 10800x.

props.code:
# solve_verify.py  —  Claude Code output
domains = [
  {"domain":"Legal",   "produce_s":0.1,  "verify_h":2.0,
   "ratio":72000, "fail_pct":40},
  {"domain":"Medical", "produce_s":2.0,  "verify_h":4.0,
   "ratio":7200,  "fail_pct":25},
  {"domain":"Finance", "produce_s":1.0,  "verify_h":3.0,
   "ratio":10800, "fail_pct":30},
]
for d in domains:
    print(f"{d['domain']}: {d['ratio']}x gap"
          f" — {d['fail_pct']}% fail if skipped")

beats.narration_text: Three domains. Same pattern — a tiny teal produce bar, a long crimson verify bar, and a gold failure-rate band below. The faster AI gets, the taller the red bar grows relative to the teal.
beats.visual_intent: Manim horizontal bar pairs: Legal, Medical, Finance — produce bar tiny teal, verify bar crimson, failure rate gold below, x-axis hour ticks

beats.narration_text: The exception that proves the rule: code with a test suite. The test suite is the verification layer — it runs in seconds and catches failure before production.

props.command: claude "For code with comprehensive tests: estimate AI solve time vs test-suite verify time. How does this compare to the legal/medical gap?"

beats.narration_text: Code with tests: verify time is less than or equal to produce time. That is not a loophole — it proves the rule. The verification layer must be designed in, not added after.
beats.visual_intent: slate: code-with-tests as the design counterexample — verify <= produce

beats.narration_text: The solve-verify gap is an engineering constraint, not a preference. Design the verification layer with the same resources as the solve layer.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Design verification like you design the solver — it is not optional.'

beats.narration_text: Your move: for your next AI-assisted task, measure the solve time and estimate the verification time. If the ratio exceeds a hundred to one, the verification layer needs to be built into the workflow.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) measure solve time, 2) estimate verify time, 3) if ratio >100x, build verification in

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The solve-verify gap is an engineering constraint, not a preference. Design the verification layer with the same resources as the solve layer. Your move: for your next AI-assisted task, measure the solve time and estimate the verification time. If the ratio exceeds a hundred to one, the verification layer needs to be built into the workflow.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [The Solve-Verify Gap: Speed Widens the Asymmetry] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
